drain_pacing_change.explanation: Pass null to _drainPendingQuestReveals so it clears the queue but does NOT fire reveals (per discoveryBridge.js line 110: `if (!scene || typeof scene.revealNode !== 'function') return drained;`). Then schedule revealNode via Phaser's time.delayedCall with a 700 ms initial delay and 380 ms stagger. Includes scene-active guard to handle the case where the player leaves the map during the delay window.

before.alpha_pulse: chained: alpha→0.5 over 140 ms (35% of 400) Sine.easeIn, then onComplete → alpha→1 over 260 ms (65%) Sine.easeOut

after.alpha_pulse: single yoyo tween: alpha→0.5 over 192 ms (35% of 550) Sine.easeIn, yoyo back over 192 ms with yoyoEase Sine.easeOut
after.shape: alpha pulse begins first (eye catches brightening), 80 ms later scale pop kicks; pulse and scale overlap

play_reveal_animation_change.rationale: yoyo: true with yoyoEase replaces the chained onComplete pattern. Total alpha-down + alpha-up duration is 384 ms, slightly less than the new 550 ms scale duration, so pulse naturally overlaps with the back-half of the scale tween rather than blinking before it. Cleaner code (no nested tween), and the visual difference vs. the previous chained pattern is sub-perceptual (same easings, same 35/65-equivalent shape just expressed via yoyo).

manual_verification_scenarios: Fresh save with bridge_collapse pre-active → start game → walk to bike (or however the map opens first) → open World Map. Expected: map renders fully (terrain, paths, home node, fog) and sits visible for ~700 ms with no node movement, THEN copper_mine pops in with a 550 ms Back.easeOut scale + concurrent alpha pulse (brightens slightly first ~80 ms before the pop). SFX should fire on the reveal. First-discovery dialog should still appear after.

Save with multiple queued reveals (e.g. quest with 2+ DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS entries pending) → load → open World Map. Expected: 700 ms initial pause, then reveals fire in queue order spaced 380 ms apart (e.g. node A at t=700, node B at t=1080). Each reveal individually shows the same 80 ms alpha-lead + 550 ms scale pop. They should NOT all fire on the same frame.

Open World Map with queued reveal, then immediately close/leave the scene before the 700 ms timer fires. Expected: no console error, no orphaned tween. Reopening the map should NOT replay the reveal (queue was already drained on first open — this is correct, not a regression). revealNode is idempotent against already-revealed tiles in any case.

constraints_observed: No new module-scope constants — INITIAL_DELAY, STAGGER, DURATION, SCALE_DELAY all inline in their methods
revealNode behavior unchanged — still runs revealArea + saveGame + redrawFog + spawn + animate + SFX + first-discovery dialog. Only WHEN it's called moved.
SFX call inside revealNode untouched (audio layer is reserved for a future agent)
HMR registration at file tail untouched
No visual emphasis added (no glow, no highlight, no new color tweens) — that's the next agent's slice
No camera movement added — that's the third agent's slice
